slots.rod: GGC MatchMonster 9'10" (Match Rod, 11-35 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Short leader for panfish — Bluegill and Crappie feed at shallow to mid-depths. Fluorocarbon is less visible than your current braid mainline and provides the stealth needed in Rocky Lake's clear water. Keep it short since panfish strike close to the float.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 3-7 ft. Panfish cruise in the shallows near structure and weed edges.

issues: Hook #4/0 is still too large for Rocky Lake panfish — Bluegill and Crappie have small mouths
No leader attached — braid mainline running directly to hook spooks fish in clear water
Braid line on a float setup can spook trout in clear lake water
Match rod's 11-35 Lb line weight is overkill for the small panfish this bait targets
improvements: Add a fluorocarbon leader (3-5 Lb, 6-10 in) between mainline and hook — critical for clear water float fishing
Downsize hook to #6 or #8 to match slot 4's improvement — you already did this on slot 4, apply it here too
Switch to mono or fluoro line for less line visibility on float rigs
Consider switching bait to Crickets (like slot 4) for broader species coverage including trout
Alternatively, switch bait to Maggots for better cost efficiency while keeping same panfish coverage

slots.rod: JigWinner 8'10" SE (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-13 Lb, Fast)
issues: Very minor: #1/0 hook is slightly large for smaller trout, but still effective
improvements: Excellent setup — the Nano Spoon 1/6 Oz is a great downsized choice from the previous 1/4 Oz Casting Spoon
Light rod + fluoro line + small spoon is the ideal trout combo for Rocky Lake
Try slow, erratic retrieves with occasional pauses — triggers reaction strikes from trout
This is your best slot — no changes needed

slots.rod: Loki 7'6" SE (Spinning Rod, Heavy, 5/7-2 1/3 Oz, 6-24 Lb, Moderate)
slots.terminal_tackle: Thorn Jighead 5/8 Oz. #3/0 with Shad 3" soft bait
issues: Heavy power rod is still significantly overpowered for Rocky Lake — max fish is ~4.9 lb
5/8 Oz Thorn Jighead is a small improvement over the old 3/4 Oz but still too heavy for this shallow lake
Braid .009" with no leader is visible to trout in clear water
#3/0 hook on the jighead is oversized for Rocky Lake fish
improvements: Switch to a medium or medium-light rod — the Heavy rod wastes sensitivity on small fish
Downsize to 1/4 Oz or 3/8 Oz jighead for better action in shallow water
Add a fluorocarbon leader (4-8 Lb) between braid and jighead to reduce visibility
Try a 2" soft plastic for better match to forage size at Rocky Lake
Consider swapping to a setup more like slot 5 or 6 — those light rods are much better matched to this lake

leader_recommendation.why: Medium leader length for versatile panfish/trout targeting. Crickets attract both shallow-feeding panfish and mid-depth trout, so a 10-16 in leader lets the bait sit in the active zone for both. Fluorocarbon is nearly invisible in water and has the right sink rate for natural bait presentation. The lighter 2-4 Lb test matches the OmniFloat's 3-6.5 Lb line weight for a balanced setup.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 5-10 ft. Start at 7 ft and adjust — if catching mostly panfish, go shallower; if targeting trout, go deeper. At Ferntastic Fishing, try 7-8 ft near the weed line.

issues: Durability is getting low (16385/18000) — keep an eye on rod condition
Very minor: mono line is slightly more visible than fluoro, but acceptable for float fishing
No leader attached — adding a fluorocarbon leader would improve bite rate especially for wary trout
improvements: HUGE improvement from last loadout — Hook #6 and Crickets is exactly right for Rocky Lake
Add a fluorocarbon leader (2-4 Lb, 10-16 in) for better stealth — especially helps with trout
Crickets cover both panfish (Bluegill, Crappie) AND trout species — excellent versatility
Light match rod with mono line and proper hook size is textbook float fishing for this lake
Consider repairing the rod before durability drops further
This is now one of your best setups — model slot 1 after this configuration

slots.rod: Aurora 8'2" Sport (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-1/2 Oz, 3-10 Lb, Fast)
issues: Minor: 1/4 Oz is at the higher end for this lake — could go lighter for finicky fish
#1/0 hook is adequate but a #1 would be slightly better for the smaller species
improvements: EXCELLENT replacement for the old CGT MonSpin X-Heavy — night and day difference
Light rod + fluoro .009" + 1/4 Oz spoon is well-matched for Rocky Lake trout
The Baitless Narrow Spoon has a tight wobble action that trout love
Try varying retrieve depth — Colorado Golden Trout often hold at mid-depth
Good complement to slot 2's Nano Spoon — gives you two different spoon profiles to try

slots.rod: ValueSpin 6'3" (Spinning Rod, Light, 1/6-5/8 Oz, 3-6.5 Lb, Fast)
issues: Mono .010" is slightly more visible than fluoro — could reduce bites from wary trout
Durability is moderate (17648/18000) — relatively new rod
6'3" is on the shorter side — less casting distance but good for accuracy
improvements: GREAT replacement for the old CosmoCast X-Heavy — perfectly suited for Rocky Lake
Nano Spinner 1/5 Oz produces vibration and flash that attracts trout and bass
Inline spinners are excellent for covering water quickly to find active fish
Consider upgrading to fluoro line when possible — trout in clear water respond better to invisible line
The short rod makes this ideal for fishing tight spots near shore at Ferntastic Fishing

slots.rod: MCT McCarp 13'2" (Match Rod, 9-25.5 Lb, Mod Fast)

leader_recommendation.why: Longer leader for dedicated trout targeting. Trout at Rocky Lake tend to hold deeper and are more line-shy than panfish. The longer 14-20 in leader keeps the Flies bait further from the float and mainline, giving a more natural presentation. Since the McCarp's mainline is already fluoro .012", the leader provides an extra stealth zone with thinner diameter near the hook. The 4-8 Lb test weight handles trophy Cutthroat Trout (up to 4.9 lb) and Colorado Golden Trout (up to 3.3 lb) while staying light enough for good bite detection.
leader_recommendation.depth_setting: Set float depth to 7-13 ft. Colorado Golden Trout and larger Cutthroat often hold in deeper water. At Trout Galore, start at 10 ft and work your way up. During peak bite times (check bite charts), trout may come shallower — adjust to 7 ft.

issues: 13'2" carp rod is very long for a small lake — harder to manage at close range
Hook #1/0 is improved from #6/0 but still a bit large for trout — #2 or #4 would be ideal
No leader attached — the long rod can cast further but trout at distance are even more line-shy
Rod's 9-25.5 Lb line weight is overpowered for trout up to 4.9 lb
improvements: BIG improvement — Flies are a top bait for all three trout species at Rocky Lake including Colorado Golden Trout ($201/kg)
Add a fluorocarbon leader (4-8 Lb, 14-20 in) — the longer leader gives Flies a natural drift below the float
Hook #1/0 is functional but downsizing to #2 or #4 would increase bite rate for trout
Fluoro .012" mainline is a good choice for trout — low visibility
This is now a solid trout-targeting float rig — the main weakness is the oversized rod
When budget allows, a 9-11' match rod would be more appropriate for this lake

passive_fishing.stand_spot_why: Deeper water access with trout holding at mid-depth. The open bank allows good rod stand placement with clear sightlines to all 3 floats. Colorado Golden Trout ($200/kg) and Cutthroat Trout ($165/kg) are the primary passive targets here.
passive_fishing.active_fishing_zone: Cast spinning rods along the shore near your rod stand. Stay within 30-40 ft. Target trout with spoons (slots 2, 5) and spinner (slot 6) while float rods soak. Alternate between Trout Galore's deeper water and the shallower weed edges for panfish.
strategy: Set TripleFeeder Rod Stand at Trout Galore — deploy 3 float rods at staggered depths
Slot 1 (MatchMonster + Bloodworms + #4/0): Target deeper trout at 8-10 ft — DOWNSIZE HOOK TO #6 FIRST
Slot 4 (OmniFloat + Crickets + #6): Cast to 5-7 ft for mixed panfish and trout — versatile depth
Slot 7 (McCarp + Flies + #1/0): Cast to 7-10 ft targeting Colorado Golden Trout ($200/kg) — DOWNSIZE HOOK TO #2-#4
Actively fish with slot 2 (Nano Spoon) casting to deeper water for Rainbow and Cutthroat Trout
When a float goes under, switch rods immediately — trout bites are steady pulls, panfish are quick dips
Colorado Golden Trout on slot 7 is your highest-value passive catch — prioritize re-baiting this rod
Cycle between active casting and checking floats every 30-60 seconds
income_estimate: Passive stand rods: Primarily trout ($165-200/kg) with occasional panfish
Active spinning: Rainbow Trout ($170/kg, up to 4.9 lb) and Cutthroat ($165/kg, up to 4.9 lb)
Colorado Golden Trout from passive rod: ~$200/kg x 1.5kg avg = $300 per catch
Estimated passive income: $400-$800/hr from stand rods

notes: FishCastle L keepnet is good for storing catches — make sure to fill it with high-value trout before selling
TripleFeeder Rod Stand allows 3 rods deployed simultaneously — ideal for float fishing multiple spots
BigBox L rod case supports your 7 rod loadout
Vault 7 tackle box provides adequate storage for current gear
Consider upgrading vest for additional carry capacity as your tackle collection grows
Groundbait mixing is available but no recipes are set up yet
For Rocky Lake, groundbait is not essential — float and spinning setups work well without it
Groundbait becomes more important at higher-level locations with feeder fishing
If you want to experiment, a simple bread-based groundbait can attract panfish to your float spot

missing_setups: Bottom fishing rig for White Sucker — use Marshmallows or Semolina Balls on a bottom/ledger setup to complete species coverage
xp_optimization: Catch ALL 8 species for first-catch XP bonuses — you're still missing White Sucker coverage
Target trophy Cutthroat Trout (4.9 lb max) and Colorado Golden Trout (3.3 lb max) — trophies give 2-3x XP
Your new light spinning setups (slots 5 & 6) qualify for 'challenge catch' XP bonuses on bigger fish
Fish both spots (Ferntastic Fishing and Trout Galore) for location discovery XP
Unique Colorado Golden Trout gives a significant first-catch bonus — your slot 7 with Flies is ideal for this
Vary your techniques between float (slots 1,4,7), spoon (slots 2,5), spinner (slot 6), and jig (slot 3) for technique variety XP bonuses
Deploy all 3 float rods on the TripleFeeder Rod Stand for passive XP while you actively cast with spinning setups
income_optimization: Colorado Golden Trout at $201/kg is the top earner — slot 7 with Flies is perfectly set up for this
Rainbow Trout ($170/kg, up to 4.9 lb = $374 max per fish) is your best total income per catch — target with slots 2, 5, 6
Cutthroat Trout ($165/kg, up to 4.9 lb = $363 max per fish) — slots 2, 4, 5, 6, 7 all cover this
Your loadout now has 5 slots capable of catching the top 3 money fish — huge improvement
Fill FishCastle L keepnet with trout before selling — maximizes silver per trip
Flies (slot 7) and Crickets (slot 4) are cheap baits that target high-value fish — great profit margin
Use spinning setups (slots 2, 5, 6) for active fishing between float rod bites for maximum catch rate
Get the unlimited Basic license ($5 BC) if you plan to fish here repeatedly
priority_changes: Fix slot 1: Downsize hook from #4/0 to #6 (like you already did on slot 4) and switch bait to Crickets — this one fix would make slot 1 as good as slot 4
Fix slot 3: This Heavy rod + 5/8 Oz jighead is still overpowered — swap to a medium-light rod if you have one, and use a 1/4 Oz jighead
Fine-tune slot 7: Downsize hook from #1/0 to #2 or #4 for better trout bite rate
Add a bottom rig slot for White Sucker using Marshmallows to complete species coverage (8/8 species)
Consider swapping slot 6's mono line to fluoro for better trout stealth
Add a fluorocarbon leader to slot 3 between the braid mainline and jighead

loadout_progress.improvement_summary: Massive improvement across the board. Slots 4, 5, and 6 went from ratings of 5, 2, and 3 to 9, 8, and 8 respectively. Hook sizes fixed on slots 4 and 7. Two X-Heavy rods replaced with proper light spinning setups. Bait choices significantly improved with Crickets and Flies replacing Semolina Balls and Dried Locusts. Only slot 1 and slot 3 still need attention.
